items.title: US stocks plunge: Nasdaq posts worst single-day point drop ever after strong jobs data
items.content: S&P 500 fell 2.64% to 7,383.74, Nasdaq plunged 4.18% to 25,709.43 after May nonfarm payrolls surged well above expectations at 110,000 vs 95,000 expected, raising fears the Fed will keep rates higher for longer. Dow fell 1.35% to 50,866.78.

items.title: US market hits record highs early in week before sharp reversal
items.content: S&P 500 closed at 7,609.78 (24th record close of 2025-26), Nasdaq at 27,093.90, Dow at 51,307.79 on June 2, extending further to 51,561.93 on June 4. But all gains were wiped out by Friday's selloff.

items.title: US April PCE inflation accelerates to 3.8% YoY, core stays at 3.3%
items.content: Headline PCE rose 0.4% MoM, YoY accelerated to 3.8% from 3.5% in March. Core PCE at 3.3% YoY, consistent with prior month. Personal spending rose $111.1B, saving rate fell to 2.6%. Inflation remains sticky well above Fed's 2% target.

items.title: HSI hangs at 24,962 as market awaits June rebalance
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed at 24,961.95 on June 5. Three companies entering implementation phase for semi-annual rebalance effective June 8. Hong Kong Q1 GDP surged 5.9% YoY — strongest in nearly 5 years — driven by AI-related electronics exports (+42.9% in April).

items.title: Hong Kong April exports surge 42.9% on AI electronics demand
items.content: Merchandise exports saw massive 42.9% YoY surge driven by global AI chip and electronics demand. Retail sales up 8.6% YoY. Unemployment steady at 3.7%. Government maintains full-year GDP forecast at 2.5-3.5%, StanChart raised its forecast to 4.3%.

items.title: US-Iran fragile ceasefire holding but tensions persist around Strait of Hormuz
items.content: Fragile ceasefire under strain with intermittent exchanges of fire. Iran accuses US of striking coastal radar sites; US says strikes were in response to Iranian attacks on commercial vessels. Negotiations ongoing with Pakistan mediating, possible 60-day ceasefire extension and reopening of Strait of Hormuz pending final approval.

items.title: Oman resists US pressure to sever ties with Iran amid Hormuz crisis
items.content: Oman insists its negotiations with Tehran are focused on lawful management of Strait of Hormuz. The crisis has already caused significant energy price spikes globally.

items.title: China semiconductor/AI stocks surge on index rebalancing and domestic demand
items.content: CSI Semiconductor Index advanced, STAR 50 showing strong YTD gains. Chinese exchanges overhauling benchmark indices to include more AI and semiconductor companies. Cambricon, Moore Threads, SMIC, Hua Hong all seeing gains. YMTC and CXMT advancing IPO plans on AI memory demand.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 beats: EPS $1.28 vs $1.23 estimate, revenue $8.94B
items.content: Revenue up 1.9% YoY to $8.94B, beating $8.82B estimate. Driven by gaming, computing, mobile phones and services. Reiterated full-year FY27 guidance with revenue $41.2-42.1B. Dollar Tree also beat with EPS $1.74 vs $1.53 estimate and raised full-year guidance.

items.title: Snowflake Q1 revenue surges 33% to $1.39B, raises full-year forecast
items.content: Revenue $1.39B (+33% YoY), product revenue $1.33B (+34% YoY). AI products driving strongest sequential dollar growth in company history. Raised full-year product revenue forecast to $5.84B (+31% YoY).

items.title: Central bank week ahead: ECB June 11, FOMC June 15-17, BoE June 18
items.content: ECB expected to hike 25bp to 2.25% on June 11 in response to energy prices and Hormuz crisis. FOMC expected to hold at 3.5-3.75% on June 17 but may remove 'next move is cut' language from statement. BoE meeting June 18 with rate at 3.75%.

items.title: Strong May jobs data reshapes rate path expectations
items.content: US added well above-expected 110,000 jobs in May, unemployment steady at 4.3%. Higher-than-expected wage growth reignites inflation concerns. Markets repricing probability of Fed rate cuts lower. Fed funds futures now pricing higher-for-longer scenario.
